---
name: frontend-design
title: Frontend Design
description: "Use when building web components, landing pages, dashboards, React components, or any production-grade frontend interface with high design quality."
---

## frontend-design

## Verification Checklist

- [ ] Environment and dependencies are properly configured
- [ ] Frontend Design output was generated successfully
- [ ] Output meets expected quality and style requirements
- [ ] Any errors during execution were resolved
- [ ] Final result is saved or delivered as expected

## Description

Frontend design skill for creating distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use when building web components, pages, artifacts, or applications.

## When to Use

Use this skill when:

- Building web components
- Creating landing pages
- Designing dashboards
- Building React components
- Creating HTML/CSS layouts
- Styling and beautifying web UI
- Building web applications

**Triggers**: "Build a component", "Create a landing page", "Design this UI", "Make it look good", "Build a dashboard", "Create a web app"

## When NOT to Use

- For code review (use requesting-code-review instead)
- For debugging (use systematic-debugging instead)
- For simple static HTML (use web-artifacts-builder instead)
- For design systems (use brand-guidelines instead)

## Workflow

### Phase 1: Understand Requirements

- What is the component/page for?
- Who are the users?
- What devices/browsers?
- What's the user flow?

### Phase 2: Design

- Sketch layout
- Choose color scheme
- Select typography
- Plan responsive behavior

### Phase 3: Implement

- Build HTML structure
- Apply CSS styling
- Add interactivity
- Optimize performance

### Phase 4: Verify

- Test on multiple devices
- Check accessibility
- Verify performance
- Get user feedback

## Tools & References

**Related Skills**:

- brand-guidelines - Apply brand colors/typography
- web-artifacts-builder - Build complex artifacts
- theme-factory - Apply themes to artifacts
- mermaid-diagrams - Visualize layouts

**Frontend Frameworks**:

- React, Vue, Svelte
- Tailwind CSS, Bootstrap
- shadcn/ui components

## Best Practices

- **Mobile-first**: Design for mobile, enhance for desktop
- **Accessibility**: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance
- **Performance**: Optimize images, minimize CSS/JS
- **Consistency**: Follow design system
- **User testing**: Validate with real users
